source.multipart_basis: Decomposition inferred from the original prompt; no rulebook citations added beyond those carried by the original sample.

full_prompt:
Design a single-span balsa/paperboard bridge model for a collegiate structural-design competition, delivered as a multi-part assembly because a bridge of this class is by construction a built-up structure composed of two truss planes (top and bottom chords + web diagonals), a deck, cross bracing, and adhesive-bonded joints, and the deflection-under-load (L/300) and slender-member buckling pass/fail both reference the assembled truss + deck + bracing behavior. Span 1200 mm between simple supports; point load 40 kg (~392 N) at midspan.

Assembly layout. (A) Top chord: upper compression member along each truss plane (2 planes total). (B) Bottom chord: tension chord along each truss plane. (C) Diagonal web members: balsa struts/ties triangulating top and bottom chords in each plane. (D) Deck: lightweight paperboard deck >= 1000 mm long spanning between truss planes and carrying the point load into the top chord / panel points. (E) Cross bracing: transverse bracing between truss planes for lateral stability. (F) Adhesive joints: bonds at every chord-web and deck-truss interface.

Material. Allowed: balsa wood (along-grain E = 3.4 GPa, density = 160 kg/m^3), lightweight paperboard, adhesive. Prohibited: metal rods, carbon fiber, synthetic structural laminate.

Load cases. LC1: 40 kg (392 N) static point load at midspan top of deck; simple supports (pin + roller) 1200 mm apart.

Pass/fail criteria (all assembly-level). (R1) Self-weight <= 1.6 kg. (R2) Midspan deflection under LC1 <= L/300 = 4.0 mm. (R3) First buckling mode load factor at LC1 >= 1.5 (slender compression chord / diagonal member stability).

Assembly policy. Self-weight (R1) sums chord + web + deck + bracing + adhesive masses. Deflection (R2) is a composite stiffness property of the full truss + deck + bracing system. Buckling (R3) typically occurs in slender compression members whose critical load is conditioned by the diagonal web restraint geometry and by deck bracing of the top chord - an assembly-scope property. No single part evaluated in isolation has meaningful pass/fail.

Solver and analysis expectations. Primary: linear static FEA (beam + shell mix) for R2. Linear buckling eigenvalue for R3. Balsa modeled as linear elastic orthotropic. Vibration, thermal, fluid, ionizing-radiation NOT required.

Deliverables. STEP AP242 of assembled bridge, bill of materials per part family, total self-weight in grams, midspan deflection at 40 kg in mm, and first buckling load factor.
short_prompt:
Design a single-span balsa/paperboard bridge model for a collegiate structural-design competition, delivered as a built-up multi-part assembly: top and bottom chords (two truss planes) + balsa diagonal web members + paperboard deck + cross bracing + PVA/adhesive joints. Span 1200 mm between simple supports (pin + roller); point load 40 kg (~392 N) at midspan top of deck. Allowed materials: balsa wood (along-grain E = 3.4 GPa, density 160 kg/m^3), lightweight paperboard, adhesive. Prohibited: metal rods, carbon fiber, synthetic structural laminate.

Load cases.
- LC1: 40 kg (392 N) static midspan.

Pass limits: self-weight <= 1.6 kg; midspan deflection under LC1 <= L/300 = 4.0 mm; first buckling load factor at LC1 >= 1.5.

prompt.title: 1200 mm balsa bridge model as multi-part assembly
prompt.brief: 1.2 m span balsa/paperboard bridge: top chord + bottom chord + diagonals + deck + cross bracing; 40 kg midspan load; L/300 deflection, buckling factor >= 1.5, self-weight <= 1.6 kg.

parts.name: top_chord
parts.role: upper compression chord of a truss plane

parts.name: bottom_chord
parts.role: lower tension chord of a truss plane

parts.name: diagonal_web
parts.role: diagonal strut/tie triangulating chords in a truss plane

parts.name: deck
parts.role: paperboard deck spanning truss planes >= 1000 mm long

parts.name: cross_bracing
parts.role: transverse bracing between truss planes for lateral stability

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: Generated coverage gate: ensures prompt-level envelope, body-count, report-field, and part geometry constraints are represented by an explicit geometry audit experiment.

interfaces.load_path: axial load transfer through truss joints; buckling boundary for chord
point-load transfer into truss via deck
lateral restraint of top chord; buckling mode reduction

fem_check_policy.rationale: Deflection (R2) is a whole-structure stiffness quantity; buckling load factor (R3) depends on chord slenderness with diagonal/deck/bracing restraints; self-weight (R1) sums all part families.

notes.why_multi_part_is_inherent: A bridge model is an intrinsic assembly of chords, webs, deck, and bracing; the pass/fail quantities are assembly-level.

eval_coverage: span + point-load bridge FEA on assembled truss
buckling of slender members with diagonal and deck restraints

generated_eval_coverage.purpose: Add runnable CCX coverage kit for JSON-only multipart prompt.
generated_eval_coverage.note: Generated checks assert solver coverage for every FEA-grade requirement, every declared load case, and prompt-level geometry constraints; they are not a domain-specific certification model.
